groups.summary: Fix three targeted bugs: (1) no-arg constructor codegen verifier error — list(), tuple(), set() trigger verifier failure due to incorrect function signature in zero-arg builtin calls; (2) string reverse slice [::-1] returns empty string instead of reversed result — negative step handling in string_ops.rs; (3) SIGBUS crash in multi-threaded test execution — shared mutable state in runtime accessed without synchronization.
groups.requirements: list(), tuple(), set() must produce empty containers without codegen errors. 'abcdef'[::-1] must produce 'fedcba'. Multi-threaded cargo test must not SIGBUS — JIT code cache and runtime state must be thread-safe.

groups.summary: Implement keyword argument dispatch throughout the calling convention. Currently mb_call* functions and Cranelift codegen do not propagate keyword arguments to callees. This affects builtins (sorted(key=), print(sep=, end=), min(default=), int(base=)), stdlib functions, and user-defined functions with keyword-only args. Requires changes to call instruction emission, builtin function signatures, and HIR-to-MIR keyword arg lowering.
groups.requirements: sorted(key=len, reverse=True), print(sep='-', end=''), min(key=fn, default=val), int('ff', base=16), str.format(name='Bob') must all produce CPython 3.12-identical results. User-defined keyword-only args (def f(*, key)) must work.
